steps.title: Read Lady Howard's concern
steps.content: Lady Howard urges Reverend Villars not to reject the trip because it is time for Evelina to "see something of the world." She warns that young people who are too rigidly secluded can develop unrealistic ideas about the world.

steps.title: Match her implied view
steps.content: Choice A narrows the concern to the influence of other young people, which the text does not mention. Choice B invents mistrust of the Mirvans, and choice D says Villars gave Evelina his own idealistic view. The remaining choice captures Lady Howard's view that Villars has protected Evelina too much and that more experience would help her. The answer is C.
